turns.content: I'm trying to build a custom error handler in my Actix-web service but I can't figure out why the HttpResponse isn't just returning a plain string. Why does this even work this way?

The reason is that actix-web expects your errors to be serializable types so it can convert them into JSON or other formats automatically. You need to implement the ResponseError trait for your custom error type, which tells the framework how to serialize that error and what HTTP status code to send back.
But I already have a domain error struct with a message field. Why can't I just use that directly in my handler?

Because actix-web needs to know how to turn your domain error into an HTTP response, not just raw text. By implementing ResponseError you define a serialize method that produces the JSON body and a status_code field that sets the HTTP status like 400 or 500 before returning HttpResponse::BadRequest(body). Without this trait implementation, the framework wouldn't know how to construct the proper response.
Okay so I need to implement two methods on my error type. What exactly does serialize output and what should status_code return?

The serialize method must produce a serde_json::Value or any other type that implements Serialize, typically just a struct with an error message field. The status_code returns the HTTP status code like std::convert::From as i32 = 400 for bad requests. This mapping ensures your domain errors translate correctly into proper HTTP responses.
This still feels like magic to me. Why does the framework require this conversion at all instead of just passing strings through?

The framework needs consistency across your entire API so that error messages are always formatted the same way and status codes are predictable. By requiring ResponseError you ensure every error goes through a standardized pipeline where JSON serialization happens uniformly, making debugging easier since you don't have to manually format strings for different endpoints.
I understand the conversion now but I'm still confused about how actix-web actually calls this trait in my middleware. Is there a specific hook where ResponseError gets invoked?

When an error occurs, actix-web catches it and checks if your type implements ResponseError. If yes, it uses the serialize method to build the body and status_code to set headers before returning HttpResponse::Status(status, body). You can also use ErrorType which wraps any type that implements ResponseError so you don't have to implement the trait yourself for every custom error.
